name: Jon Faddis
abstract: Jon Faddis (born July 24, 1953) is an American jazz trumpet player, conductor, composer, and educator, renowned for both his playing and for his expertise in the field of music education. Upon his first appearance on the scene, he became known for his ability to closely mirror the sound of trumpet icon Dizzy Gillespie, who was his mentor…

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Slide Hampton, Jon Faddis, James Moody, myself on the front line.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Jon Faddis, Claudio Roditi. Roy Hargrove. Every night a different trumpet player. And that album came out and it was called “To Dizzy With Love.” And I had such a ball playing with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophone section had Clarence Clemons on tenor, Grover Washington Jr. on baritone sax, Joshua Redman on tenor, and Jon Faddis on trumpet... I was really glad to be a part of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: for one note, we have Jon Faddis, Victor Paz, Anthony Jackson, and Stan Getz... I can’t believe I am here to play one note with the most, the most famous musicians in the world
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I’m commissioned now and then by the Carnegie Hall Jazz Band under the direction of Jon Faddis to write an arrangement for a presentation they do at Carnegie Hall or somewhere
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Jon Faddis, Alex Foster, Bill Evans the tenor player, myself on the trombone... That was the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023
